county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Sherman County formed 1876 from Bexar. County seat is Stratford. Texas Panhandle / South Plains region with ranching and farming heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Stratford. North Panhandle county organized 1889. Sherman County Historical Commission. Coldwater Creek heritage. XIT Ranch territory. Check Bexar for pre-organization records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Sherman County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1876; County Clerk in Stratford holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Sherman County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1876 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Sherman County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1876. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Sherman County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1876. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Sherman County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1876 to 1950 for Sherman County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Sherman County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Sherman County from 1876. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Smith County formed 1846 from Nacogdoches. County seat is Tyler. East Texas / Piney Woods region with timber industry and plantation heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Tyler. East Texas county. Smith County Historical Society. Tyler Public Library genealogy collection. Camp Ford (largest Confederate POW camp west of the Mississippi) records at NARA. Significant African American community. Rose industry heritage. Check Nacogdoches for pre-1846 records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Smith County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1846; County Clerk in Tyler holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Smith County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1846 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Smith County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1846. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Smith County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1846. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Smith County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Smith County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Smith County Enslaved Persons Records -- TSLAC / County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Estate inventories listing enslaved persons, bills of sale, tax rolls listing slaveholders and enslaved persons counts, and Freedmen's Bureau records (NARA). Smith County was part of the Texas plantation economy.

source_profiles.source_name: Smith County Oil and Gas Lease Records -- GLO / County Clerk
source_profiles.repository: Texas General Land Office, Austin / County Clerk, Tyler
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Oil and gas lease records, mineral rights transfers, and related land documents from 1930. Texas GLO maintains state mineral records. County Clerk has private mineral lease records.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Smith County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Smith County from 1846. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Somervell County formed 1875 from Hood. County seat is Glen Rose. North Texas / Blackland Prairie region with farming and urban growth heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Glen Rose. North-central Texas county. Somervell County Museum. Dinosaur Valley State Park. Check Hood for pre-1875 records. Glen Rose has fossilized dinosaur tracks heritage.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Somervell County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1875; County Clerk in Glen Rose holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Somervell County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1875 to 1950 for Somervell County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Somervell County from 1875. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Starr County formed 1848 from Nueces. County seat is Rio Grande City. South Texas / Rio Grande Valley region with Spanish colonial and Mexican land grant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Rio Grande City. Rio Grande Valley county with Mexican land grant heritage. Fort Ringgold (frontier military post). Starr County Historical Society. Significant Mexican-American records. Check Nueces for pre-1848 records. Some of the oldest continuously inhabited settlements on the Texas border.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Starr County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1848; County Clerk in Rio Grande City holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Starr County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1848 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Starr County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1848. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Starr County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1848. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Starr County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Starr County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Spanish Colonial / Mission Records -- Starr County Area
source_profiles.repository: Catholic Archives of San Antonio / TSLAC / Bexar Archives at UT-Austin
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Spanish colonial records including mission registers (baptisms, marriages, burials), presidio records, and land grants. Starr County area was part of the Spanish colonial system. Records at Catholic Archives of San Antonio, TSLAC, and Bexar Archives at UT-Austin.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Colonial-era records vary in survival; many originals at Catholic Archives or in Mexican archives

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Starr County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Starr County from 1848. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Stephens County formed 1858 from Bosque. County seat is Breckenridge. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Breckenridge. Central-west Texas county organized 1876. Swenson Memorial Museum. Breckenridge oil boom (1920s). Check Bosque for pre-organization records. Stephens County Historical Association.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Stephens County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1858; County Clerk in Breckenridge holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Stephens County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1858 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Stephens County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1858. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Stephens County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1858. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Stephens County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1858 to 1950 for Stephens County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Native American Records -- Stephens County Area (various tribes)
source_profiles.repository: NARA, Fort Worth / Oklahoma Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Pre-settlement and frontier-era records for various tribes in the Stephens County area. Includes Indian Affairs records, military campaign records, and reservation records at NARA Fort Worth. Texas frontier counties experienced significant Native American conflict through the 1870s.

source_profiles.source_name: Stephens County Oil and Gas Lease Records -- GLO / County Clerk
source_profiles.repository: Texas General Land Office, Austin / County Clerk, Breckenridge
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Oil and gas lease records, mineral rights transfers, and related land documents from 1917. Texas GLO maintains state mineral records. County Clerk has private mineral lease records.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Stephens County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Stephens County from 1858. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Sterling City. West Texas county. Sterling County Historical Society. Ranching heritage. Check Tom Green for pre-1891 records. Sparse population.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Aspermont. Rolling Plains county organized 1888. Stonewall County Historical Commission. Double Mountain Fork area. Ranching heritage. Check Bexar for pre-organization records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Sutton County formed 1887 from Crockett. County seat is Sonora. Texas Hill Country region with German, Czech, and frontier heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Sonora. West-central Texas county organized 1890. Old Sonora Ice House Museum. Caverns of Sonora heritage. Ranching (sheep, goat, cattle). Check Crockett for pre-organization records.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Tulia. South Plains county organized 1890. Swisher County Museum. Farming (grain, cotton). Tule Canyon heritage. Check Bexar for pre-organization records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Tarrant County formed 1849 from Navarro. County seat is Fort Worth. North Texas / Blackland Prairie region with farming and urban growth heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Fort Worth. Major urban county. Fort Worth Public Library's Genealogy and Local History Department is a major repository. Tarrant County Archives. Cattle Trails Museum. Log Cabin Village. Amon Carter Museum of American Art archives. Stockyards heritage. Significant African American community (Stop Six, Como neighborhoods). Check Navarro for pre-1849 records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Tarrant County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1849; County Clerk in Fort Worth holds deed books
